rationale:
Investigated the codebase directly rather than relying on the auth-gated demo. Stack is Next.js + Prisma + PostgreSQL + Auth.js with GitHub OAuth + OpenAI SDK (@ai-sdk/openai) + @octokit/auth-app — the only submission in the batch with all four of (a) a real relational DB, (b) verified third-party OAuth, (c) verified LLM integration, and (d) explicit GitHub API integration via Octokit. The Prisma schema models cohorts, weeks, users, tasks, votes, streaks, and ship events with 40+ fields across the relations; an index on (cohort, startsAt) suggests query-pattern thinking, not just throwing tables together. The repo ships a seed.ts for development data, a vitest config for tests, and a momentum_project_brief.md documenting the design before the build. At 1.2 MB across 25 commits with the deepest domain modeling in the batch, this is enterprise-grade scaffolding by single-evening standards.

Held back from 10 because the auth wall prevents verifying the UX matches the schema's ambition, and the actual feature implementation behind the login (streak rendering, ship-event timeline, LLM hype messages) isn't visible from the code alone without running the app end-to-end. The brief asked for a PM tool with a real motivation layer; the streak + ship-event schema is the clearest 'motivation layer' implementation in the batch, but motivation is a UX property, not a schema property, and we can only verify the schema side of that promise from a code review.
